defs: to be finished; to be completed
notes: A versatile verb with many abstract extensions. In the context of entering a home, it reflects the traditional Japanese architectural feature of a raised floor. When referring to nervousness, it specifically implies 'stage fright' or being flustered. Note that while '揚がる' (to be deep-fried) and '挙がる' (to be captured/listed) share the reading 'あがる', they use different kanji.

grammar_points.usage: 上がる is intransitive (something rises on its own), whereas 上げる is transitive (someone raises something).

archetypes: どうぞ、お上がりください
grammar_points.usage: Used when inviting someone into a house or a room with a tatami/raised floor. It is more polite and culturally nuanced than using '入る'.

archetypes: レポートが書き上がる
grammar_points.usage: Attached to the stem of a verb to indicate that a task has been thoroughly completed or brought to a finish.
Specifically describes the physical and mental state of being flustered or having stage fright.

phrases.ja: どうぞ、お上[あ]がりください
phrases.en: Please, come in (step up into the house)

phrases.ja: 効果[こうか]が上[あ]がる
人前[ひとまえ]で上[あ]がる
phrases.en: to get nervous in front of people

phrases.ja: 風呂[ふろ]から上[あ]がる
phrases.en: to get out of the bath

phrases.ja: 利益[りえき]が上[あ]がる

examples.ja: 最近[さいきん]、野菜[やさい]の値段[ねだん]がどんどん上[あ]がっている。
examples.en: Lately, the price of vegetables has been rising steadily.

examples.ja: 玄関[げんかん]で靴[くつ]を脱[ぬ]いで、リビングに上[あ]がった。
examples.en: I took off my shoes at the entrance and entered the living room.

examples.ja: 本番[ほんばん]で上[あ]がってしまい、セリフを忘[わす]れてしまった。
examples.en: I got stage fright during the performance and forgot my lines.

examples.def: to be finished; to be completed
examples.ja: ようやく論文[ろんぶん]が書[か]き上[あ]がった。
毎日[まいにち]練習[れんしゅう]したおかげで、かなりテニスの腕[うで]が上[あ]がった。
examples.en: Thanks to practicing every day, my tennis skills have improved considerably.

synonyms.ja: 上昇[じょうしょう]する
